expected_main_papers.title: 3D U-Net: Learning Dense Volumetric Segmentation from Sparse Annotation
expected_main_papers.note: Provides the 3D U-Net backbone idea for volumetric and spatiotemporal generation.

expected_main_papers.title: Video Diffusion Models
expected_main_papers.note: Foundational video diffusion model framing and sampling basics.

expected_main_papers.title: Imagen Video: High Definition Video Generation with Diffusion Models
expected_main_papers.note: Shows cascaded high-definition text-to-video generation with diffusion models.

expected_main_papers.title: Make-A-Video: Text-to-Video Generation without Text-Video Data
expected_main_papers.note: Uses image-text and unlabeled video data to reduce the need for paired text-video training.

expected_main_papers.title: Align your Latents: High-Resolution Video Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
expected_main_papers.note: Moves video diffusion into latent space for more efficient high-resolution synthesis.

expected_main_papers.title: Tune-A-Video: One-Shot Tuning of Image Diffusion Models for Text-to-Video Generation
sections: 6. Adapting image diffusion models to video
expected_main_papers.note: Adapts image diffusion models to videos through one-shot tuning.

expected_main_papers.title: Text2Video-Zero: Text-to-image diffusion models are zero-shot video generators
sections: 6. Adapting image diffusion models to video
expected_main_papers.note: Shows training-free adaptation of text-to-image diffusion models to video generation.

expected_main_papers.title: Lumiere: A Space-Time Diffusion Model for Video Generation
expected_main_papers.note: Introduces a space-time diffusion architecture for coherent video generation.

expected_concept_sections.name: 1. Why video generation is harder than image generation
required_ideas: temporal consistency, motion, long-range dependency, and compute challenges
why extending image diffusion to video is nontrivial
motivate spatiotemporal architectures and efficient representations

expected_concept_sections.name: 2. Diffusion parameterization and sampling
required_ideas: video diffusion as noising/denoising over video tensors
noise/data/velocity parameterizations and sampling stability
how Video Diffusion Models adapt diffusion to temporal data

expected_concept_sections.name: 3. Architectures for spatiotemporal generation
required_ideas: 3D U-Net and space-time U-Net ideas
joint spatial and temporal modeling
architecture choices that preserve motion coherence

expected_concept_sections.name: 4. Scaling text-to-video systems
required_ideas: large-scale cascaded text-to-video generation
text conditioning and super-resolution stages
strategies using image-text and video data

expected_concept_sections.name: 5. Latent video diffusion
required_ideas: pixel-space video diffusion is expensive
latent representations reduce high-resolution video cost
temporal alignment in latent video synthesis

expected_concept_sections.name: 6. Adapting image diffusion models to video
required_ideas: one-shot tuning of image diffusion models for video
training-free or light-weight image-to-video adaptation
motion and attention modifications for temporal consistency

expected_concept_sections.name: 7. Practical synthesis
required_ideas: combine stable parameterization, spatiotemporal architectures, latent representations, and adaptation
explain tradeoffs among quality, consistency, compute, and data availability
summarize when to use each family of methods

quality_checks: HTML opens in a local HTTP server with visible styling from stylesheet.min.css
LaTeX formulas render through MathJax rather than appearing as raw TeX when formulas are present
Blog is image-rich and every included figure has a visible source-paper attribution
The blog explains paper selection and section-paper mapping in selection_note.md
The final result directory includes the HTML and all referenced assets needed for offline viewing
The blog should be a coherent paper-reading article, not just a bullet list or copied abstracts
All HTML-referenced assets resolve relative to the delivered HTML path and return 200 under a local HTTP server
